title: GameStop Q1 2023 earnings results miss revenue estimates on smaller loss than expected
text: GameStop reported Q1 2023 earnings with a loss of $0.14 per share, beating the consensus EPS estimate of a $0.17 loss. Revenue fell short of expectations, as consensus centered on a $0.17 loss per share and revenue forecasts clustered near $1.36 billion to $1.4 billion, roughly flat versus Q1 2022. The mean estimate of a $0.12 loss represented a sizable improvement from the prior year swing to a $0.52 loss per share. Analysts produced an EPS range with a high of a $0.06 loss, a mean near a $0.12 loss, and a low around a $0.24 loss. This meant the company beat the consensus EPS estimate despite missing on revenue, demonstrating improving operational efficiency even as top-line sales remained under pressure. Wedbush analyst Michael Pachter anticipated potential for GME to beat Q1 estimates, though Pachter still anticipated a slight decrease in overall revenue. Pachter maintained a Sell rating on GME stock and a price target of $6.50 per share. The turnaround narrative was still questioned by most analysts at this time.
